title: Bad Bunny's Historic Grammy Awards Win Brings in Flood of Streams
text: Bad Bunny experienced a significant surge in streaming activity following his historic Grammy Awards win, where his album Debí Tirar Más Fotos became the first Spanish-language album to be awarded Album of the Year. The data reveals remarkable growth across multiple platforms. Album streams for Debí Tirar Más Fotos increased by 240% compared to the previous Monday, accumulating over 16 million on-demand streams in the U.S. Total artist streams for Bad Bunny generated 36 million streams within a 24-hour period following the Grammy ceremony, representing a 117% boost overall. Digital sales jumped 903% to nearly 2,000 copies, with additional non-album tracks contributing 1,000 more sales for a cumulative 591% increase. When Grammy host Trevor Noah performed an English translation of the song DtMF, the track saw a 273% streaming increase, climbing from 733,000 to over 2.7 million streams. Bad Bunny is set to make additional history by becoming the first artist to headline the Super Bowl halftime show entirely in Spanish.
